explanation: The requirement specifies that the system must both measure and display the percentage of oxygen delivered to the patient. There are atomic propositions capturing 'measureO2_PRC_' (measuring the delivered oxygen percentage) and 'displayO2_PRC_' (displaying the delivered oxygen percentage). To ensure these functions are always active (i.e., as an invariance over all system operation), we use 'G (measureO2_PRC_ & displayO2_PRC_)', which is the global (always) property. This LTL property assures that, at every point in system operation, both oxygen percentage is being measured and displayed.

The requirement states that the system must measure and display the percentage of oxygen in the gas delivered to the patient. This implies two atomic propositions must both be true: 'measureO2_PRC_' (the percentage of oxygen is measured) and 'displayO2_PRC_' (the percentage of oxygen is displayed). These two items should be continually satisfied (i.e., globally true) during operation. So, we use the template for globally ('G') requiring the conjunction of these two atomic propositions. Thus, the property G(measureO2_PRC_ & displayO2_PRC_) ensures that at all times, the system is both measuring and displaying the percentage of oxygen being delivered to the patient.

The requirement is for the system to both measure and display the percentage of oxygen in the gas delivered to the patient. This means that at all times, when the system is operating, both measurement and display of O2 percentage should be occurring, not just once, but as a persistent property. The best way to express this in LTL is with a globally ("G") operator ensuring that whenever the system is operating, the atomic propositions 'measureO2_PRC_' and 'displayO2_PRC_' are both true. Thus, at every state, both the measurement and display of O2 percentage are maintained. The conjunction (AND) operator '&' is used to represent both actions happening together.

The requirement is that the system shall measure and display the percentage of oxygen in the gas delivered to the patient. Both actions (measurement and display) must occur. The atomic propositions 'measureO2_PRC_' and 'displayO2_PRC_' encode these actions. The property expresses that globally (at all times), whenever the system measures the oxygen percentage ('measureO2_PRC_'), it must also display it ('displayO2_PRC_'). The implication (measureO2_PRC_ -> displayO2_PRC_) is a boolean expression relating these two propositions. This formalizes the requirement that the display accompanies the measurement, as specified in the natural language requirement.

The input sentence requires that the system must both measure and display the percentage of oxygen being delivered to the patient. The atomic propositions 'measureO2_PRC_' and 'displayO2_PRC_' directly capture these two requirements. Since the two must happen concurrently, the most straightforward temporal property is that it should always be the case (globally) that if oxygen percentage is measured ('measureO2_PRC_'), then it is also displayed ('displayO2_PRC_'). This is captured by the implication in the 'Globally, if A then B' template (index 30), with the LTL property G (measureO2_PRC_ -> displayO2_PRC_). This means at any time if oxygen percentage is measured, then it must also be displayed, capturing the requirement that both measurement and display must be realized by the system.

The requirement is that the system must both measure and display the percentage of oxygen being delivered to the patient. This means that during system operation, whenever the system is functioning, both measurement and display of the percentage oxygen must be ensured. Among the atomic propositions, 'measureO2_PRC_' captures measurement, and 'displayO2_PRC_' captures display of the percentage of delivered oxygen. The appropriate temporal property is that globally (at all times), whenever oxygen concentration is measured, it should also be displayed. This is well captured by the template: G (measureO2_PRC_ -> displayO2_PRC_), which means 'it is always the case that if the oxygen percentage is measured, then it is displayed.' This captures the intent that measurement without display is not sufficient; display should accompany any measurement. N/A for N_DURATION as this property is unconditional and applies globally.

The requirement states that the system must both measure and display the percentage of oxygen being delivered to the patient. This can be captured in LTL by requiring that both 'measureO2_PRC_' and 'displayO2_PRC_' always eventually hold, i.e., they should both always be true (globally). Using the template for 'globally (bool_exp3)', and setting bool_exp3 to the conjunction of these two atomic propositions, we express that at all times, the percentage of oxygen is being measured and displayed as required.

The requirement is that the system must both measure AND display the percentage of oxygen being delivered to the patient. This corresponds to the atomic propositions 'measureO2_PRC_' (for measurement) and 'displayO2_PRC_' (for display). Since the requirement is a persistent one (the system shall do this, generally at all times it is running and in a relevant context), the best LTL match is to require that globally (at all times), both measuring and displaying are true. This can be captured by 'G (measureO2_PRC_ & displayO2_PRC_)', which uses the template 'G bool_exp3' with bool_exp3 set to 'measureO2_PRC_ & displayO2_PRC_'.

The input requirement states that the system shall measure AND display the percentage of oxygen being delivered to the patient. In the given atomic propositions, 'measureO2_PRC_' stands for 'measure the percentage of oxygen,' and 'displayO2_PRC_' stands for 'display the percentage of oxygen.' Therefore, to capture that BOTH must always occur during operation, we select a template that ensures 'measureO2_PRC_' AND 'displayO2_PRC_' are always true. The LTL template 'G (bool_exp3)' (index 7) with 'measureO2_PRC_ & displayO2_PRC_' as the boolean expression represents this: it requires globally (at all times) that both measurement and display of oxygen percentage are maintained. This aligns with the system's requirement for continuous measurement and display of oxygen percentage for patient safety.

The requirement states that the system shall measure and display the percentage of oxygen in the delivered gas. Thus, both 'measureO2_PRC_' and 'displayO2_PRC_' need to be true, and this must always hold for correct operation. The best direct LTL translation ensuring both are true (possibly simultaneously, or at every step) is the conjunction of both, hence 'G (measureO2_PRC_ & displayO2_PRC_)'. This is captured using template 7 ('G bool_exp3'), with bool_exp3 = 'measureO2_PRC_ & displayO2_PRC_'. This fits the intent of the requirement and the given AP descriptions: 'measureO2_PRC_' is for measurement, and 'displayO2_PRC_' is for showing it to the operator.
